conbody.p: Overview: The Assistant Usage Summary helps the CDI Managers get details about the AI suggestions in CDS Assistant and their acceptance and rejections rates. This helps to analyze the usage of CDS Assistant and CDS engagement with this tool.
By default, the report displays data for last 3 calendar months based on the admission date.
Navigation: On the Analytics home page left side panel, navigate to CDE Triage Triage and CDS Assistant Usage Assistant Usage Summary .

The Assistant Usage Summary dashboard displays the following charts:
Documented Diagnoses
Documented Procedures
Documentation Opportunities: Diagnoses
Documentation Opportunities: Clinical Evidences
On the top panel, you can select desired values for the slicers (refer ) and the corresponding data gets filtered in the charts.
For filters on all pages, refer .

This chart allows report users to gain insights into Documented Diagnoses and CDS activity by displaying the count of diagnoses, whether the CDS removed, accepted, or left the suggestions unaddressed.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Includes all encounter statuses except ‘Review Not Needed; The visual shows the data for ‘Suggested’ Documented Diagnoses by default. For data on Quality and Other types of Documented Diagnoses, use the 'Suggestion Type’ slicer.
conbody.note: Each bar for Documented Diagnoses represents the total number of those diagnoses presented in CDS Assistant (100%), and each segment within the bar represents the percentage of diagnoses with a specific CDS action. If a bar has segments for Accepted (60%), Removed (30%), and Unaddressed (10%), it means that out of all the diagnoses presented, 60% are accepted, 30% are removed, and 10% are Unaddressed. (Accepted + Removed + Unaddressed = Total Documented Diagnoses presented in CDS Assistant).

row.entry: The count and percentage of Documented Diagnoses that were accepted by the CDS in CDS Assistant.

ul.li: Y-axis displays the Documented Diagnoses in the format: ICD 10 Code: Documented Diagnoses Description. (E.g. L97.909: Non pressure chronic ulcer of unspecified part of unspecified leg with unspecified severity). Only display the first 15-20 characters, including the ICD 10 code, and show the rest of the description in a tooltip upon hover.
X-axis displays Accepted, Removed, and Unaddressed metrics.

row.entry: The count and percentage of Documented Diagnoses that were removed by the CDS in CDS Assistant.
The count and percentage of Documented Diagnoses that were unaddressed by the CDS

conbody.p: This chart allows report users to gain insights into documented procedures and CDS activity by displaying the count of procedures, whether the CDS removed, accepted, or left the suggestions unaddressed.
Info Icon(Text on hover): Encounter Status: Includes all encounter statuses except ‘Review Not Needed; The visual shows the data for ‘Suggested’ Documented Procedures.
conbody.note: Each bar for Documented Procedures represents the total number of those procedures presented in CDS Assistant (100%), and each segment within the bar represents the percentage of procedures with a specific CDS action. If a bar has segments for Accepted (60%), Removed (30%), and Unaddressed (10%), it means that out of all the procedures presented, 60% are accepted, 30% are removed, and 10% are Unaddressed. (Accepted + Removed + Unaddressed = Total Documented Procedures presented in CDS Assistant).

row.entry: The count and percentage of Documented Procedures that were accepted by the CDS in CDS Assistant.

li.p: Y-axis displays the Documented Procedures in the format: ICD10 PCS Code: Documented Procedures Description or Procedure Description (for SNOMED Procedures) (E.g. 0RG2: Fusion cervical vertebral joints, Debridement of wound of skin). Only display the first 15-20 characters and show the rest of the description in a tooltip upon hover.
X-axis displays Accepted, Removed, and Unaddressed metrics.

row.entry: The count and percentage of Documented Procedures that were removed by the CDS in CDS Assistant.
The count and percentage of Documented Procedures that were unaddressed by the CDS.

conbody.p: This chart allows report users to get insights into Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses and CDS activity by displaying the count of diagnoses, whether the CDS removed, accepted, or left the suggestions unaddressed.
Info Icon(Text on hover): Encounter Status: Includes all encounter statuses except ‘Review Not Needed; The visual shows the data for ‘Suggested’ Documentation Opportunities: Diagnoses.
conbody.note: Each bar for Documentation Opportunities: Diagnoses represents the total number of those diagnoses presented in CDS Assistant (100%), and each segment within the bar represents the percentage of diagnoses with a specific CDS action. If a bar has segments for Accepted (60%), Removed (30%), and Unaddressed (10%), it means that out of all the Documentation Opportunities: Diagnoses presented, 60% are accepted, 30% are removed, and 10% are Unaddressed. (Accepted + Removed + Unaddressed = Total Documentation Opportunities: Diagnoses presented in CDS Assistant).

row.entry: The count and percentage of Documented Opportunities Diagnoses that were accepted by the CDS in CDS Assistant.

li.p: Y-axis displays the Documentation Opportunities: Diagnoses in the format: ICD 10 Code: Documentation Opportunities: Diagnoses Description (E.g., N17.9: Acute kidney failure, unspecified). Only display the first 15-20 characters, including the ICD 10 code, and show the rest of the description in a tooltip upon hover.
X-axis displays Accepted, Removed, and Unaddressed metrics.

row.entry: The count and percentage of Documented Opportunities Diagnoses that were removed by the CDS in CDS Assistant.
The count and percentage of Documented Opportunities Diagnoses that were unaddressed by the CDS.

conbody.p: This chart provides report users with insights into Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidences, displaying their counts and indicating whether they were attributed by the CDS, attributed by the system, removed, or left unaddressed.
Info Icon(Text on hover): Encounter Status: Includes all encounter statuses except ‘Review Not Needed; The visual shows the data for ‘Suggested’ Documentation Opportunities: Clinical Evidences.
conbody.note: Each bar for Documentation Opportunities: Clinical Evidences represents the total number of those evidences presented in CDS Assistant (100%), and each segment within the bar represents the percentage of evidences with a specific action. If a bar has segments for CDS Attributed (30%), System Attributed (30%), Removed (30%), and Unaddressed (10%), it means that out of all the Documentation Opportunities: Clinical Evidences presented, 30% are CDS attributed, 30% are system attributed, 30% are removed, and 10% are Unaddressed. (CDS Attributed+ System Attributed + Removed + Unaddressed = Total Documentation Opportunities: Clinical Evidences presented in CDS Assistant).

row.entry: The count and percentage of Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidences that were attributed by the CDS.

li.p: Y-axis displays the Documentation Opportunities: Clinical Evidences in the format: Documentation Opportunities: Clinical Evidences Description (E.g., Gram-Negative Blood Culture). Only display the first 15-20 characters, including the ICD 10 code, and show the rest of the description in a tooltip upon hover.
X-axis displays CDS Attributed, System Attributed, Removed, and Unaddressed metrics.

row.entry: The count and percentage of Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidences that were attributed by the system.
The count and percentage of Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidences that were removed by the CDS in CDS Assistant.
The count and percentage of Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidences that were unaddressed by the CDS.

li.p: Allows user to select the admit date range.
Format: Between date range, example: 3/1/2023 - 6/30/2023.

ul.li: Default: Last 3 calendar months date range is selected by default.
Displays the discharge date range.

li.p: Format: Between date range, example: 4/1/2023 - 6/30/2023.

ul.li: With the Reset button, you can reset both the admit and discharge date range.
Tooltip: Ctrl + Click to reset both Admit Date and Discharge Date.
Displays both active and inactive facilities in alphabetical order that you have access to.
You can select multiple facilities.
You can search the facility by Facility ID or Description.
Format: Facility Description (Facility ID).
Default: ALL.
Displays both active and inactive payers in alphabetical order associated with the facilities you have access to.
You can select multiple payers.
"Unassigned Payer" displays data for the encounters that do not have a payer assigned.
You can search the Payer by Payer ID or Description.
Format: Payer Description (Payer ID).
Default: ALL.

li.p: Displays both active and inactive CDSs in alphabetical order associated with the facilities user has access to.
User can select multiple CDSs
"Unassigned CDS" displays data for the encounters that do not have a CDS assigned.
Users can search the CDS by CDS ID or Name.
Format: CDS Name (CDS ID).
Default: ALL.

ul.li: This filter displays the list of available units.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Unit.
Default: ALL.

row.entry:
Displays the below options:
Documented Diagnoses
Documented Procedures
DO: Diagnoses
DO: Clinical Evidence
User can select multiple Assistant categories.
Default: ALL.

Displays the below options:
Accepted
Removed
Unaddressed
CDS Attributed
System Attributed
User can select multiple activities.
Default: ALL.

Displays the below options:
Suggested
Quality
Other
User can select multiple suggestion types.
Default: Suggested.

li.p: Displays all standard and custom ranks that can be assigned to the encounters. This slicer filters the data based on the highest standard/custom rank assigned to an encounter (where 1 is the highest standard rank and 11 is the lowest standard rank).
User can select multiple ranks.
Users can search the rank number.
“Unranked” displays data for all encounters that are not ranked.
Format: Rank # ( such as Rank 1, Rank 2).
Default: ALL.

entry.p: Displays the list of diagnoses and procedures along with the code.
Diagnoses Code format: ICD 10 Code: Diagnoses description.
Procedure Code format: ICD 10 PCS Code: Documented Procedures description.
The slicer gets updated dynamically based on the Assistant Category selected.

ul.li: Displays the available patient types. Example: Inpatient, Outpatient.
Default: Inpatient.

li.p: Displays the list of available admit service. Admit Service is the provider group/service area where the patient was admitted to, such as Cardiology or Orthopedics.
User can select multiple Admit Services.
Users can search the Admit Service.
Default: ALL.

ul.li: Displays the available patient types.Example: ED, IP, etc.
You can select multiple Visit Type.
You can search the Visit Type.
Default: ALL.

li.p: Displays the available options for Weekend Only Stay: Y and N.
User can select more than one option.
Weekend Only Stay = Y (Unassigned encounters admitted after Friday, 6PM and Discharged before Sunday, 11.59 PM).
Default: ALL.
